mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion, which drives consumption and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% do not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal a contraction in both supply and labor demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that are larger than either univariate move alone.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to produce a lower mean 20-day return than either condition in isolation (difference in means t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals that interest-rate-sensitive demand is cracking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, month-over-month declines in HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or the signal Sharpe ratio is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures consumer spending momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through revenue surprise channels.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 52% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) reduces real earnings expectations and increases the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes exceeding 0.4% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real GDP (GDPC1) relative to the prior quarter signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with a falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a recessionary shock that forces the Federal Reserve to ease into a weakening economy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings estimates get cut faster than discount rates fall.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term fails to add explanatory power beyond the univariate terms (F-test p > 0.05).
